prose_fallback.decision_handoff_excerpt:
# Decision summary

Post-synthesis view: Yes is still more likely than No, but only modestly. I estimate a 0.65 to 0.69 probability that the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute candle on April 17 closes strictly above 74,000. The swarm’s mild-bearish-to-market stance holds up after spot verification, but the edge versus market is small and only medium-weakly verified because this is a single-minute settlement with ordinary BTC volatility still fully capable of flipping the outcome.

## Why this may matter now

Market-implied baseline in the task was 0.715, while a fresh Polymarket fetch showed the 74,000 line closer to 65% at synthesis time. My syndicated range is 0.65 to 0.69. That makes the edge unclear-to-marginal rather than actionable with confidence. The likely reason for any residual mispricing is that traders may still over-anchor to BTC being above 74k now while underweighting exact-minute settlement fragility; but because the live market snapshot itself appears lower than the task snapshot, much of the original apparent No edge may already have compressed.

## Shift versus swarm baseline

The provisional swarm center was about 0.68. My final range is effectively centered n …

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: The market's roughly 72% Yes price is broadly reasonable because BTC is already trading above 74k on Binance and recent range supports the threshold, but exact-minute settlement makes the contract slightly less favorable than spot alone suggests.
main_logical_chain: Market implies about 71.5% Yes and neighboring strikes suggest a plausible near-term BTC distribution.
Direct Binance data shows BTC already above 74k and recently trading above that level repeatedly.
Because the contract is about one exact minute rather than a daily close, some probability discount versus naive spot anchoring is warranted.
That leaves a final estimate near but slightly below market at 69%.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT spot was about 74,792 at research time, already above the 74,000 threshold.
Recent Binance daily candles repeatedly traded and closed above 74k.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on a single 12:00 ET one-minute candle close, so ordinary volatility could still produce a No even if BTC stays near current levels.
Recent intraday trading already dipped below 74k, showing the threshold is not safely out of range.
unresolved_ambiguities: No independent external catalyst check was needed, but near-term macro or crypto-specific shocks could still alter the path materially.
Formal settlement references the Binance website candle rather than the API endpoints used for context.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: A sustained move back below 74k before settlement or evidence of elevated downside volatility would move the estimate toward or below 50%, while stable trading well above 75k would increase confidence in Yes.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: The contract resolves about 41 hours after research time and depends on the exact 12:00 ET April 17 one-minute close, so short-horizon timing noise is central.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Good case-fit but only medium-low independence: Polymarket defines the contract and Binance provides direct exchange context on the named pair; source-of-truth ambiguity is low.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: BTC is already modestly above 74k on Binance, so Yes is more likely than not, but the market overstates that edge because settlement depends on a single noon ET 1-minute close and ordinary crypto volatility can still flip the result.
main_logical_chain: The market resolves strictly on Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close on Apr. 17 being above 74,000.
Current Binance price is only modestly above the threshold, so Yes deserves to be favored but not treated as near-certain.
Because BTC commonly traverses 1-2% over 1-2 day windows, the outside view discounts the market's 71.5% optimism.
That yields a mildly Yes estimate around 64%.
strongest_supports: Binance spot price during research was about 74.8k, already above the 74k threshold.
Binance 24-hour weighted average was about 74.26k, suggesting the threshold is not being cleared by only a transient spike.
strongest_disconfirmers: Recent 24-hour range already crossed below 74k, and a routine 1-2% move lower would make No correct.
Settlement uses one specific noon ET 1-minute candle, which increases path fragility relative to a day-close style market.
unresolved_ambiguities: Minor operational ambiguity remains because the rule text cites the Binance UI while verification used API/docs, though this seems low-risk.
The exact volatility regime over the next ~41 hours remains unknown.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: A move materially below 74k before settlement would push the view toward No; sustained trade well above roughly 75.5k or a calmer regime would push the estimate higher; exchange-specific anomalies would reduce confidence.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Timing is central: the contract resolves on the Apr. 17 12:00 ET one-minute Binance candle close, not on a daily average or end-of-day price.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Primary source quality is high because Binance is the named settlement source; Polymarket rules provide useful contract verification, but independence is only medium-low because direct pricing evidence is concentrated in Binance.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: Lean Yes, but the edge is capped by narrow Binance settlement-window risk rather than broad BTC direction.
main_logical_chain: Polymarket rules say settlement is the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 PM ET 1-minute candle close on April 17.
Direct Binance checks show BTC/USDT currently above 74000 and recent minute closes above the threshold.
Because the contract is narrow and time-specific, current in-the-money status is supportive but not decisive.
That supports a moderate Yes estimate below the market's embedded confidence.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance API check showed BTCUSDT at 74807.29, above the threshold.
Recent sampled Binance 1-minute closes were also above 74000.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact Binance 1-minute close, so a modest downswing can still flip the market to No.
The current cushion above 74000 is only about 1.1%, small relative to routine BTC volatility.
unresolved_ambiguities: How large the cushion above 74000 will be by settlement morning.
Whether volatility into the final hours is large enough to overwhelm the current edge.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would move up if BTC holds comfortably above 74000 into April 17 morning with a larger cushion; I would move down if Binance BTC/USDT slips back toward or below 74000 or if settlement-morning minute closes weaken.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Very high: the market resolves on the April 17, 2026 12:00 PM ET Binance 1-minute candle close, which maps to 16:00:00 UTC.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Good for a medium-difficulty case: governing contract rules were checked on Polymarket, and the settlement venue plus kline mechanics were verified directly via Binance API.
